title: Research
text:
Our research focuses on understanding how environmental variation and global change influence biodiversity, species movement, and ecosystem functioning across spatial and temporal scales.
We use integrative approaches combining ecology, geospatial technology, and quantitative modelling.

title: Research
text:
Our research focuses on understanding how environmental variation and global change influence biodiversity, species movement, and ecosystem functioning across spatial and temporal scales.
We use integrative approaches combining ecology, geospatial technology, and quantitative modelling.

title: Research
text:
Research Themes

1. Global change ecology
We study how species distribute and move across space and time, and how climate and land cover change reshape these patterns and processes.



2. Movement ecology under human influence

title: Research
section: We use satellite tracking and spatial models to study bird/mammal habitat use, migration, and their responses to environmental change.
text:
We use satellite tracking and spatial models to study bird/mammal habitat use, migration, and their responses to environmental change.

3. Biodiversity in human-dominated landscapes
We quantify how environmental heterogeneity and its sptiotemporal dynamics shape biodiversity patterns and ecosystem processes.



4. Spatial epidemiology
We study how environmental conditions influence disease patterns in wildlife and humans.

title: Research
text:
Applications
Our work supports:

Biodiversity conservation planning

Migratory species protection

Ecosystem management under climate change

Understanding wildlife disease emergence

title: Si Lab
text:
We are a spatial ecology research group focusing on understanding how environmental change shapes species distributions and movement, biodiversity patterns, and ecosystem processes across space and time.
Our work integrates ecological theory with geospatial and computational methods to address challenges in global environmental change.

title: Si Lab
text:
We are a spatial ecology research group focusing on understanding how environmental change shapes species distributions and movement, biodiversity patterns, and ecosystem processes across space and time.
Our work integrates ecological theory with geospatial and computational methods to address challenges in global environmental change.

title: Si Lab
text:
Research Focus
We study:

Environmental drivers of species distributions and biodiversity patterns

Movement ecology and migratory behaviour of birds
Impacts of global change on ecological patterns and processes
Spatial epidemiology of disease in wildlife and humans

title: Si Lab
text:
Approach
We combine:

Satellite tracking and biologging

Remote sensing and GIS

Spatial statistical modelling

Mathematical and ecological modelling

Long-term ecological datasets

title: Si Lab
text:
Mission
To understand ecological systems in a changing world and develop evidence-based strategies for biodiversity conservation and sustainable ecosystem management.

title: Si Lab
text:
Join Us
We welcome students and collaborators interested in spatial ecology, conservation biology, and global change science.
